templates.title: Calibre Behind Traefik
templates.name: calibre-traefik
templates.description: Calibre behind Traefik with a shared library volume.

env.name: CLI_ARGS
env.label: Optionally pass cli start arguments to calibre.

templates.stackfile:
services:
  {{APP_NAME}}:
    image: lscr.io/linuxserver/calibre:latest
    container_name: {{APP_NAME}}
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      TZ: {{APP_TZ}}
      PUID: {{APP_PUID}}
      PGID: {{APP_PGID}}
      CUSTOM_USER: {{CUSTOM_USER}}
      PASSWORD: {{PASSWORD}}
      CLI_ARGS: {{CLI_ARGS}}
    volumes:
      - {{APP_NAME}}_config:/config
      - {{APP_NAME}}_library:/books
    networks:
      - proxy
    labels:
      - traefik.enable=true
      - traefik.docker.network=proxy
      - traefik.http.routers.{{APP_NAME}}.rule=Host(`{{APP_SUBDOMAIN}}.{{APP_DOMAIN}}`)
      - traefik.http.routers.{{APP_NAME}}.entrypoints=web
      - traefik.http.services.{{APP_NAME}}.loadbalancer.server.port=8080

volumes:
  {{APP_NAME}}_config:
  {{APP_NAME}}_library:

networks:
  proxy:
    external: true

templates.title: Calibre-Web Behind Traefik
templates.name: calibre-web-traefik
templates.description: Calibre-Web behind Traefik using an existing Calibre library.

env.name: DOCKER_MODS
env.label: #optional & **x86-64 only** Adds the ability to perform ebook conversion

env.name: OAUTHLIB_RELAX_TOKEN_SCOPE
env.label: Optionally set this to allow Google OAUTH to work

templates.stackfile:
services:
  {{APP_NAME}}:
    image: lscr.io/linuxserver/calibre-web:latest
    container_name: {{APP_NAME}}
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      TZ: {{APP_TZ}}
      PUID: {{APP_PUID}}
      PGID: {{APP_PGID}}
      DOCKER_MODS: {{DOCKER_MODS}}
      OAUTHLIB_RELAX_TOKEN_SCOPE: {{OAUTHLIB_RELAX_TOKEN_SCOPE}}
    volumes:
      - {{APP_NAME}}_config:/config
      - {{LIBRARY_VOLUME}}:/books
    networks:
      - proxy
    labels:
      - traefik.enable=true
      - traefik.docker.network=proxy
      - traefik.http.routers.{{APP_NAME}}.rule=Host(`{{APP_SUBDOMAIN}}.{{APP_DOMAIN}}`)
      - traefik.http.routers.{{APP_NAME}}.entrypoints=web
      - traefik.http.services.{{APP_NAME}}.loadbalancer.server.port=8083

volumes:
  {{APP_NAME}}_config:
  {{LIBRARY_VOLUME}}:
    external: true

networks:
  proxy:
    external: true

templates.title: Changedetection.io Behind Traefik
templates.name: changedetection.io-traefik
templates.description: changedetection.io provides free, open-source web page monitoring, notification and change detection.

env.name: BASE_URL
env.label: Specify the full URL (including protocol) when running behind a reverse proxy

env.name: PLAYWRIGHT_DRIVER_URL
env.label: Specify the full URL to your chrome driver instance. See the [wiki](https://github.com/dgtlmoon/changedetection.io/wiki/Playwright-content-fetcher) for details.

templates.stackfile:
services:
  {{APP_NAME}}:
    image: lscr.io/linuxserver/changedetection.io:latest
    container_name: {{APP_NAME}}
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      TZ: {{APP_TZ}}
      BASE_URL: {{BASE_URL}}
      PLAYWRIGHT_DRIVER_URL: {{PLAYWRIGHT_DRIVER_URL}}
    volumes:
      - {{APP_NAME}}_config:/config
    networks:
      - proxy
    labels:
      - traefik.enable=true
      - traefik.docker.network=proxy
      - traefik.http.routers.{{APP_NAME}}.rule=Host(`{{APP_SUBDOMAIN}}.{{APP_DOMAIN}}`)
      - traefik.http.routers.{{APP_NAME}}.entrypoints=web
      - traefik.http.services.{{APP_NAME}}.loadbalancer.server.port=5000

volumes:
  {{APP_NAME}}_config:

networks:
  proxy:
    external: true

templates.title: Code Server Behind Traefik
templates.name: code-server-traefik
templates.description: code-server is VS Code running on a remote server, accessible through the browser. - Code on your Chromebook, tablet, and laptop with a consistent dev environment. - If you have a Windows or Mac workstation, more easily develop for Linux. - Take advantage of large cloud servers to speed up tests, compilations, downloads, and more. - Preserve battery life when you're on the go. - All intensive computation runs on your server. - You're no longer running excess instances of Chrome.

env.name: HASHED_PASSWORD
env.label: Optional web gui password, overrides `PASSWORD`, instructions on how to create it is below.

env.name: SUDO_PASSWORD
env.label: If this optional variable is set, user will have sudo access in the code-server terminal with the specified password.

env.name: SUDO_PASSWORD_HASH
env.label: Optionally set sudo password via hash (takes priority over `SUDO_PASSWORD` var). Format is `$type$salt$hashed`.

env.name: PROXY_DOMAIN
env.label: If this optional variable is set, this domain will be proxied for subdomain proxying. See [Documentation](https://github.com/coder/code-server/blob/main/docs/guide.md#using-a-subdomain)

env.name: DEFAULT_WORKSPACE
env.label: If this optional variable is set, code-server will open this directory by default

env.name: PWA_APPNAME
env.label: If this optional variable is set, the PWA app will the specified name.

templates.stackfile:
services:
  {{APP_NAME}}:
    image: lscr.io/linuxserver/code-server:latest
    container_name: {{APP_NAME}}
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      TZ: {{APP_TZ}}
      HASHED_PASSWORD: {{HASHED_PASSWORD}}
      SUDO_PASSWORD: {{SUDO_PASSWORD}}
      SUDO_PASSWORD_HASH: {{SUDO_PASSWORD_HASH}}
      PROXY_DOMAIN: {{PROXY_DOMAIN}}
      DEFAULT_WORKSPACE: {{DEFAULT_WORKSPACE}}
      PWA_APPNAME: {{PWA_APPNAME}}
    volumes:
      - {{APP_NAME}}_config:/config
    networks:
      - proxy
    labels:
      - traefik.enable=true
      - traefik.docker.network=proxy
      - traefik.http.routers.{{APP_NAME}}.rule=Host(`{{APP_SUBDOMAIN}}.{{APP_DOMAIN}}`)
      - traefik.http.routers.{{APP_NAME}}.entrypoints=web
      - traefik.http.services.{{APP_NAME}}.loadbalancer.server.port=8443

volumes:
  {{APP_NAME}}_config:

networks:
  proxy:
    external: true

templates.title: Heimdall Behind Traefik
templates.name: heimdall-traefik
templates.description: heimdall is a way to organise all those links to your most used web sites and web applications in a simple way.  Simplicity is the key to Heimdall.  Why not use it as your browser start page? It even has the ability to include a search bar using either Google, Bing or DuckDuckGo.

env.name: ALLOW_INTERNAL_REQUESTS
env.label: By default, Heimdall blocks lookup requests to private or reserved IP addresses, if your instance is not exposed to the internet, or is behind some level of authentication, you can set this to `true` to allow requests to private IP addresses.

templates.stackfile:
services:
  {{APP_NAME}}:
    image: lscr.io/linuxserver/heimdall:latest
    container_name: {{APP_NAME}}
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      TZ: {{APP_TZ}}
      ALLOW_INTERNAL_REQUESTS: {{ALLOW_INTERNAL_REQUESTS}}
    volumes:
      - {{APP_NAME}}_config:/config
    networks:
      - proxy
    labels:
      - traefik.enable=true
      - traefik.docker.network=proxy
      - traefik.http.routers.{{APP_NAME}}.rule=Host(`{{APP_SUBDOMAIN}}.{{APP_DOMAIN}}`)
      - traefik.http.routers.{{APP_NAME}}.entrypoints=web
      - traefik.http.services.{{APP_NAME}}.loadbalancer.server.port=80

volumes:
  {{APP_NAME}}_config:

networks:
  proxy:
    external: true

templates.title: Jellyfin Behind Traefik
templates.name: jellyfin-traefik
templates.description: jellyfin is a Free Software Media System that puts you in control of managing and streaming your media. It is an alternative to the proprietary Emby and Plex, to provide media from a dedicated server to end-user devices via multiple apps. Jellyfin is descended from Emby's 3.5.2 release and ported to the .NET Core framework to enable full cross-platform support. There are no strings attached, no premium licenses or features, and no hidden agendas: just a team who want to build something better and work together to achieve it.

env.name: JELLYFIN_PublishedServerUrl
env.label: Set the autodiscovery response domain or IP address, include http(s)://.

templates.stackfile:
services:
  {{APP_NAME}}:
    image: lscr.io/linuxserver/jellyfin:latest
    container_name: {{APP_NAME}}
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      TZ: {{APP_TZ}}
      JELLYFIN_PublishedServerUrl: {{JELLYFIN_PublishedServerUrl}}
    volumes:
      - {{APP_NAME}}_config:/config
      - {{APP_NAME}}_data_tvshows:/data/tvshows
      - {{APP_NAME}}_data_movies:/data/movies
    networks:
      - proxy
    labels:
      - traefik.enable=true
      - traefik.docker.network=proxy
      - traefik.http.routers.{{APP_NAME}}.rule=Host(`{{APP_SUBDOMAIN}}.{{APP_DOMAIN}}`)
      - traefik.http.routers.{{APP_NAME}}.entrypoints=web
      - traefik.http.services.{{APP_NAME}}.loadbalancer.server.port=8096

volumes:
  {{APP_NAME}}_config:
  {{APP_NAME}}_data_tvshows:
  {{APP_NAME}}_data_movies:

networks:
  proxy:
    external: true

templates.title: Openssh Server Behind Traefik
templates.name: openssh-server-traefik
templates.description: openssh-server is a sandboxed environment that allows ssh access without giving keys to the entire server. Giving ssh access via private key often means giving full access to the server. This container creates a limited and sandboxed environment that others can ssh into. The users only have access to the folders mapped and the processes running inside this container.

env.name: PUBLIC_KEY
env.label: Optional ssh public key, which will automatically be added to authorized_keys.

env.name: PUBLIC_KEY_FILE
env.label: Optionally specify a file containing the public key (works with docker secrets).

env.name: PUBLIC_KEY_DIR
env.label: Optionally specify a directory containing the public keys (works with docker secrets).

env.name: PUBLIC_KEY_URL
env.label: Optionally specify a URL containing the public key.

env.name: SUDO_ACCESS
env.label: Set to `true` to allow `linuxserver.io`, the ssh user, sudo access. Without `USER_PASSWORD` set, this will allow passwordless sudo access.

env.name: PASSWORD_ACCESS
env.label: Set to `true` to allow user/password ssh access. You will want to set `USER_PASSWORD` or `USER_PASSWORD_FILE` as well.

env.name: USER_PASSWORD
env.label: Optionally set a sudo password for `linuxserver.io`, the ssh user. If this or `USER_PASSWORD_FILE` are not set but `SUDO_ACCESS` is set to true, the user will have passwordless sudo access.

env.name: USER_PASSWORD_FILE
env.label: Optionally specify a file that contains the password. This setting supersedes the `USER_PASSWORD` option (works with docker secrets).

env.name: LOG_STDOUT
env.label: Set to `true` to log to stdout instead of file.

templates.title: Qbittorrent Behind Traefik
templates.name: qbittorrent-traefik
templates.description: The qbittorrent project aims to provide an open-source software alternative to µTorrent. qBittorrent is based on the Qt toolkit and libtorrent-rasterbar library.

env.name: WEBUI_PORT
env.label: for changing the port of the web UI, see below for explanation

env.name: TORRENTING_PORT
env.label: for changing the port of tcp/udp connection, see below for explanation

templates.stackfile:
services:
  {{APP_NAME}}:
    image: lscr.io/linuxserver/qbittorrent:latest
    container_name: {{APP_NAME}}
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      TZ: {{APP_TZ}}
      WEBUI_PORT: {{WEBUI_PORT}}
      TORRENTING_PORT: {{TORRENTING_PORT}}
    volumes:
      - {{APP_NAME}}_config:/config
      - {{APP_NAME}}_downloads:/downloads
    networks:
      - proxy
    labels:
      - traefik.enable=true
      - traefik.docker.network=proxy
      - traefik.http.routers.{{APP_NAME}}.rule=Host(`{{APP_SUBDOMAIN}}.{{APP_DOMAIN}}`)
      - traefik.http.routers.{{APP_NAME}}.entrypoints=web
      - traefik.http.services.{{APP_NAME}}.loadbalancer.server.port=8080

volumes:
  {{APP_NAME}}_config:
  {{APP_NAME}}_downloads:

networks:
  proxy:
    external: true

templates.title: Sonarr Behind Traefik
templates.name: sonarr-traefik
templates.description: sonarr (formerly NZBdrone) is a PVR for usenet and bittorrent users. It can monitor multiple RSS feeds for new episodes of your favorite shows and will grab, sort and rename them. It can also be configured to automatically upgrade the quality of files already downloaded when a better quality format becomes available.
templates.stackfile:
services:
  {{APP_NAME}}:
    image: lscr.io/linuxserver/sonarr:latest
    container_name: {{APP_NAME}}
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      TZ: {{APP_TZ}}
    volumes:
      - {{APP_NAME}}_config:/config
      - {{APP_NAME}}_tv:/tv
      - {{APP_NAME}}_downloads:/downloads
    networks:
      - proxy
    labels:
      - traefik.enable=true
      - traefik.docker.network=proxy
      - traefik.http.routers.{{APP_NAME}}.rule=Host(`{{APP_SUBDOMAIN}}.{{APP_DOMAIN}}`)
      - traefik.http.routers.{{APP_NAME}}.entrypoints=web
      - traefik.http.services.{{APP_NAME}}.loadbalancer.server.port=8989

volumes:
  {{APP_NAME}}_config:
  {{APP_NAME}}_tv:
  {{APP_NAME}}_downloads:

networks:
  proxy:
    external: true
